summary: The right rewrite makes a recommendation and justifies it with source-of-truth logic, while the left memo stays overly balanced.
strengths_right: Clarifies the split between source-of-truth docs and lighter collaboration
Explains why repo-local docs are more trustworthy
risks_right: Could acknowledge one stronger tradeoff if expanded
trace_notes_left: No trace sidecar is provided for the left baseline.
trace_notes_right: Right candidate includes a full decision trace anchored in recommendation logic.
The trace makes the source-of-truth rationale explicit before rewriting.
cognitive_notes_left: Left candidate has no cognitive artifact showing how the recommendation would be engineered.
cognitive_notes_right: Right candidate includes a CW artifact with recommendation-focused baseline, modules, and QA fields.
The process discipline matches the stronger decision quality in the prose.
